seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Semantic Kernel Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Semantic Kernel agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Semantic Kernel agents, AI hallucinations manifest as fabricated outputs from plugins, invalid plans from the planner, or corrupted memory recalls that mislead execution. These issues cascade through plugin chaining and cross-plugin data flows, producing unreliable agent behavior in enterprise applications. Vex integration via kernel filters enables real-time detection of these hallucinations specific to SK's patterns.

howItManifests.symptom: Subsequent plugins receive hallucinated data from prior plugin outputs, like fake API responses
howItManifests.impact: Chain fails with unrecoverable errors or propagates incorrect data to user-facing responses

howItManifests.symptom: Planner invents non-existent functions or incorrect parameter sequences in execution plans
howItManifests.impact: Agent crashes during plan execution or executes dangerous unintended operations

howItManifests.symptom: Semantic memory retrieves fabricated conversation history or non-existent prior context
howItManifests.impact: Agent loses context continuity and provides responses contradicting actual user history

howItManifests.symptom: One plugin's hallucinated output pollutes shared kernel variables used by other plugins
howItManifests.impact: Inconsistent state across agent execution leads to erratic multi-turn behavior

detection.name: Plugin Result Hallucination Check
detection.description: Detect hallucinations in plugin function results by validating against function schema and known good outputs
detection.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.contents import ChatHistory

kernel = Kernel()

@kernel.on_function_invocation
async def detect_plugin_hallucination(func_result: str, func_name: str) -> bool:
    # Check if result contains hallucinated patterns
    hallucination_patterns = ['I don\'t know', 'As an AI', 'Sorry I can\'t']
    if any(pattern in func_result for pattern in hallucination_patterns):
        raise ValueError(f"Hallucination detected in {func_name}: {func_result}")
    # Schema validation
    if func_name == 'get_weather':
        if not func_result.startswith('temp='):
            raise ValueError('Invalid weather format')
    return True

detection.name: Plan Validity Verification
detection.description: Validate planner-generated execution plans contain only registered functions

detection.name: Memory Recall Consistency Check
detection.description: Verify semantic memory recalls match actual chat history timestamps and content

fixes.name: Fallback Function Invocation
fixes.description: Replace hallucinated plugin results with safe fallback functions

fixes.name: Plan Repair with Registered Functions
fixes.description: Automatically repair invalid plans by replacing hallucinated functions

fixes.name: Memory Grounding with Chat History
fixes.description: Ground memory recalls against verified chat history to prevent fabrication
fixes.code:
from semantic_kernel.chatter import ChatHistory

async def ground_memory_recall(memory_result: str, chat_history: ChatHistory) -> str:
    # Verify memory against actual chat history
    history_text = " ".join([m.content for m in chat_history])
    
    if memory_result not in history_text:
        # Use verified history instead
        grounded_result = chat_history[-3:][0].content if chat_history else "No verified context"
        print("⚠️ Memory hallucination corrected")
        return grounded_result
    return memory_result

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a Semantic Kernel filter on function results, automatically detecting hallucinations across plugin chains, planner outputs, and memory operations. It uses SK-specific patterns like function invocation metadata and plan step validation to catch issues before they cascade. Vex provides real-time remediation with fallback functions and auto-repair of execution plans.

The Vex SDK (`pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk`) adds zero-boilerplate filters that inspect every kernel function result against semantic, structural, and domain-specific ground truth. For enterprise SK agents, Vex maintains hallucination baselines per-plugin and auto-generates repair functions.

Configuration is declarative - define hallucination detectors once, and Vex handles chaining, memory contamination, and planner validation across your entire agent surface area.
vexAutomation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
import vex

# Install: pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk
kernel = Kernel()

# Vex auto-integration as kernel filter
@vex.hook(kernel=kernel)
def sk_hallucination_detector(result: str, metadata: dict) -> bool:
    """Semantic Kernel hallucination detector"""
    func_name = metadata.get('function_name')
    
    # Plugin-specific validation
    if func_name == 'get_weather' and not result.startswith('temp='):
        return False
    
    # Generic hallucination patterns
    hallucination_signals = ['As an AI', 'I cannot', 'Sorry']
    if any(signal in result for signal in hallucination_signals):
        return False
    
    return True

# Auto-remediation
@vex.repair(kernel=kernel)
def sk_hallucination_fix(result: str, metadata: dict) -> str:
    func_name = metadata.get('function_name')
    if func_name == 'get_weather':
        return "temp=72°F, sunny"  # Ground truth fallback
    return "Operation completed successfully"

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK now and eliminate Semantic Kernel hallucinations in one line of code: `pip install vex-sdk`
